goal: Emit depth as float32 EXR meters (or 16-bit mm), not 16-bit normalized-to-scene-bounds PNG.
thoughts: Two separate annotators with identical-sounding names: distance_to_camera = 3D Euclidean distance from camera origin to hit point; distance_to_image_plane = Z-depth along the camera's view axis. Most ML depth models (MiDaS, DPT, metric-depth) expect Z-depth. PNG normalization is a writer choice — swap to EXR float32 writer OR 16-bit mm custom writer. Sky pixels: write as +inf in EXR (MiDaS friendly) or clamp to a sentinel like 65535 mm in 16-bit. Never round-trip through normalized PNG.
code:
# 1. Replace annotator with z-depth (image-plane), not Euclidean
configure_sdg(
    stage="annotators",
    annotators=[
        {"name": "rgb", "type": "rgb"},
        {"name": "depth", "type": "distance_to_image_plane"},
    ],
)

# 2. Writer: float32 EXR preserves raw meters
configure_sdg(
    stage="writer",
    writer="BasicWriter",
    params={
        "output_dir": "<DEST_PATH>/apples_depth_exr",
        "depth_format": "exr_float32_meters",
        "sky_value": "inf",                # or <CLAMP_M> for clamped sentinel
    },
)

# 3. Alternative: 16-bit mm if EXR is not in Priya's loader
configure_sdg(stage="writer", params={"depth_format": "png16_millimeters", "clamp_m": 65.5})

# 4. Verify one frame
preview_sdg(pipeline="<PIPE_ID>", frame_count=1)
lookup_knowledge(query="replicator distance_to_image_plane vs distance_to_camera exr float32")
failure_modes: assistant picks distance_to_camera when Z-depth is needed
assistant recommends post-processing normalized PNG back to meters
assistant leaves sky as 0 (confuses near-field pixels)
assistant promises disparity from depth without providing baseline
